title: review-intent — Judge the Reasoning, Not Just the Diff
logline: When an AI writes the code, the diff can't tell you if the reasoning was sound — review-intent renders git diff as one interactive page that pins the author's claimed intent against un-gameable measured blast radius, in a two-pane layout with an author-set review order, live charts, and ask-the-agent Q&A over MCP, so contradictions surface and you approve with eyes open.
note: Realized by demo/capture/promo.html (1920x1080 stage) + demo/capture/promo.mjs (Playwright director/recorder). Wall-clock design timeline ends ~56.7s; the recorded video runs ~0.9s shorter (Playwright recordVideo startup latency) and ffmpeg adds a 0.6s fade-in / 0.8s fade-out, landing the final render around 55s. Silent stage, caption-driven; live tool footage from ../widget-api/review.html composited via an app-window iframe. Captioned features reflect the two-pane review.html rework: deep-dive charts now live in <details class=deeper> (.deeper-grid); diagrams in .diagram-grid; the file spine lives in the left rail with author-set order + measured-rank badges; the ask-the-agent panel is .review-feedback. soundtrack.mjs scores every beat label emitted here.

scenes.caption:
Your agent wrote the code.
In seconds.
scenes.motion: fade-in from black, text snaps on, ominous stillness

scenes.caption:
Now you're
the bottleneck.
scenes.motion: Approve button glows, cursor creeps toward it

scenes.caption: LGTM
scenes.motion: click Approve; LGTM stamp slams over a blurred unread diff; glitch flicker

scenes.caption:
The diff shows WHAT changed.
Not whether the reasoning was sound.
scenes.motion: blurred diff lines drifting behind a dimmed overlay

scenes.caption: review-intent — See the change. Judge the intent.
scenes.motion: wordmark overshoots then lands; the synthwave DROP lands on this beat

scenes.caption:
git diff -> one interactive review page.
No LLM. No API key. No token cost.
scenes.motion: live review.html, gentle auto-scroll of the top section

scenes.caption:
MEASURED. Straight from the diff. Un-gameable.
Touches auth. Touches dependencies.
scenes.motion: ken-burns onto the two red sensitive-path badges (scorecard-crop.png)

scenes.caption:
CLAIMED, right beside it.
When they disagree, you see it.
scenes.motion: risk ledger slides in beside the measured card (split-screen, risk-ledger.png + scorecard-crop.png)

scenes.caption:
Four charts. Hand-drawn SVG. Zero deps.
Treemap, risk-vs-churn, diff mass, complexity.
scenes.motion: live iframe scrolled to .deeper-grid (the four new charts), gentle push-in

scenes.caption: One click. Reviewed in priority order.
scenes.motion: live guided tour (.tb-tour then .tour-next x2), highlight flash drives the cuts

scenes.caption:
14 themes. Live.
Same un-gameable truth.
scenes.motion: live theme switch via .tb-gear: synthwave then dark, smooth palette morphs

scenes.caption:
Meet the new review page.
Two panes — a review-order rail beside the deep dive.
scenes.motion: live iframe scrolled to top, framing the full .shell two-pane layout; open-fifth synth swell accent

scenes.caption:
Author sets the review order.
The measured rank stays visible — sink a risky file and the override is auditable.
scenes.motion: push in on the left-rail .spine; press-highlight a .file-rank-measured badge; rising rank-shuffle accent

scenes.caption:
Ask the agent — on the page.
The reviewer asks. The agent answers live. No context-switch.
scenes.motion: scroll to .review-feedback, type a question into .fb-general-input, reveal scripted live-over-MCP answer bubble; type-blip then warm confirm chime

scenes.caption:
npx @christianmorup/review-intent
MIT - open source
Stop rubber-stamping AI code.
scenes.motion: command resolves in, accent underline wipe, hold; tonic Am resolve, fade out
